reason: The text explicitly describes a conflict between Hebrew workers (בני ישראל) and the Egyptian ruling elite (Pharaoh and his government). The passage details how a new Pharaoh, concerned about the growing Hebrew population, devises strategies to oppress them ('Let us deal wisely with them'). He imposes taskmasters and forced labor upon the Hebrew people, forcing them to build storage cities with harsh work. The text states 'the Egyptians abhorred the children of Israel' (ויקצו מפני בני ישראל) and explicitly describes how the Egyptians enslaved them with harsh servitude (בפרך). The conflict escalates to the Pharaoh ordering the drowning of Hebrew male infants. This represents a clear hierarchical conflict between oppressed workers and ruling authorities.
